seoTitle: Lemington vs Lemon Tree — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lemington and Lemon Tree. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lemington vs Lemon Tree: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lemington and Lemon Tree.
heading: Lemington vs Lemon Tree — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lemington, New South Wales and Lemon Tree, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lemon Tree has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Lemington (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lemington or Lemon Tree safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lemington has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Lemon Tree scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lemington scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Lemon Tree scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lemington or Lemon Tree?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lemon Tree has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
